heading: Our story
paragraphs: InnerScope Hearing Technologies was built by a family that has spent more than five decades in hearing health. The story begins in the early 1960s, when Kimberly Moore started learning the hearing aid business from her father, Marvin Posey. In 1987, Kimberly and her husband Mark Moore co-founded their first clinic in Sacramento and spent the next 29 years building it into one of the largest independently owned hearing aid clinic networks in Northern California, growing to more than 70 audiological locations across California and Florida. Their son Matthew Moore joined in 2006, bringing a decade of senior-market marketing expertise and making the Moores a three-generation hearing health family.

In 2016, the family sold its remaining 20 clinics to Helix Hearing Care, the Widex-affiliated retail group, with cumulative proceeds from years of clinic sales exceeding 50 million dollars. With that chapter closed, the Moores turned their full attention to InnerScope, founded in 2012, and a single conviction: that quality hearing care had been priced and gatekept out of reach for most Americans, and that retail and direct-to-consumer channels could change that.

Today InnerScope (OTC: INND) and its operating company, OTCHealth Inc., focus on making over-the-counter hearing aids and senior health products available where seniors already shop, from major retailers to the pharmacy counter.
